opening_angle: Connect OpenAI's mission in safe AI with Conner’s experience building deployable insider threat systems that prioritize operational reliability, auditability, and cross-functional execution
paragraph_focus: Direct alignment with insider threat detection through endpoint automation, multi-source correlation, and remediation pipelines
Concrete examples of building secure cloud-native systems with IAM/secrets management in AWS ECS
Demonstrated responsible AI use via RAG pipelines and LLM tooling with governance — directly relevant to OpenAI’s safety-first ethos
Emphasis on documentation, guardrails, and operational handoff quality to support scalable security delivery
voice_controls: Use 'deployable controls', 'operational outcomes', and 'auditability' — avoid 'innovative', 'cutting-edge', or vague AI claims
Replace passive phrasing with active verbs like 'built', 'delivered', 'standardized'
Keep metrics qualitative unless explicitly provided; avoid invented numbers
claims_to_avoid: Saying I 'led' initiatives unless the source material confirms leadership — use 'delivered', 'collaborated on', or 'built' instead
Generic statements like 'passionate about cybersecurity' without technical grounding
Overstating AI expertise — focus on applied, auditable tooling not model development
